claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK

Child is 16 and in approved education
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your child (or children).

**First, let me ask: How old is your child?**

The age categories are:
- **Under 16** - automatic qualification (subject to other checks)
- **16 to 19** - may qualify if in approved education or training
- **20 or over** - generally not eligible

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, we can assess each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have two children I'm asking about - Alex, who's 6, and Blake, who's 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you assess eligibility for both Alex (age 6) and Blake (age 16).

Let me start with **Alex, who is 6 years old**. Since Alex is under 16, let me check the next requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Alex (age 6):**

Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)? 

(Since Alex is only 6 years old, I'm assuming Alex is not working, but I need to confirm this to proceed properly through the assessment.)

**Also, please let me know once we've covered Alex's situation, and then we'll assess Blake (age 16) separately, as Blake's assessment may involve different questions about education or training.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
No, Alex doesn't work at all - he's only 6! 

And yes, let's go through Alex first and then we can talk about Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense - Alex is only 6, so definitely not working. Let me continue with Alex's assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Alex (age 6):**

Is Alex **NOT** receiving certain benefits in their own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

(Again, since Alex is 6, I'd expect the answer to be that Alex is not receiving these benefits, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with Alex's assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Alex (age 6):**

Is Alex in any form of apprenticeship?

(Since Alex is only 6, I'm certain he's not in an apprenticeship, but I need to confirm to proceed.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - he's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with Alex's assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Alex (age 6) - Now checking YOUR eligibility as the claimant:**

Do you meet the UK residency requirements? You qualify if **ANY** of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**, or
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months), or
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (for up to 91 days), or
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources (not claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit), or
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources, or
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside, or
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do you meet any of these residency requirements?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually live in the UK - I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Let me ask a follow-up question:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant** (such as working for the UK government, diplomatic service, or armed forces), **or are you living in certain qualifying countries?**

Some people living abroad can still qualify for Child Benefit if they meet specific circumstances, such as:
- Working as a Crown servant abroad
- Living in certain countries that have agreements with the UK

Could you tell me more about your situation living abroad? What country are you in, and what is the nature of your work or reason for being there?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I'm just living abroad with my family for personal reasons, not for any government work.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying your situation. Based on what you've told me, I need to navigate to the outcome for the residency requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand your situation now. Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, I need to give you the outcome for both Alex and Blake.

**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

**The reason:** You do not meet the UK residency requirements.

**Explanation:** To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances such as:
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria like working, studying, or having sufficient resources)
- Being a Crown servant living abroad
- Living in certain qualifying countries with specific agreements

Since you're living abroad for personal reasons and don't fall into any of these special categories, you don't meet the residency requirements to claim Child Benefit - even though both Alex (age 6) and Blake (age 16) would otherwise potentially qualify based on their ages.

**What this means:** 
- This applies to both children, as the residency requirement is about YOU as the claimant, not the children
- If your circumstances change and you return to live in the UK, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time

Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about this decision?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
I understand. That's disappointing, but it makes sense - since I don't live in the UK, I can't claim the benefit. Thank you for explaining that clearly.

<thinking>
The agent has now provided me with a clear eligibility outcome - I am not eligible for Child Benefit because I don't meet the UK residency requirements. This applies to both of my children (Alex and Blake). The agent has explained the reasoning and the decision is complete.

According to my instructions, when the agent has told me if I am eligible, I should invoke the `exit_loop` tool.
</thinking>

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
You're very welcome, and I'm sorry it wasn't the outcome you were hoping for. You're absolutely right - the residency requirement is a fundamental eligibility criterion for Child Benefit, and since you're living abroad without meeting the special circumstances (like being a Crown servant), you cannot claim it at this time.

Let me now formally record the eligibility outcome for both of your children:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Just to be clear: if you do return to the UK to live, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that point, so please keep that in mind for the future.**

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please don't hesitate to reach out. Best wishes to you and your family!

reasonings: Alex (age 6) would meet the child-related qualifying criteria as he is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements, which is a mandatory criterion for claiming Child Benefit. The claimant is living abroad for personal reasons and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not in a qualifying country. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex.

Blake (age 16) was not fully assessed as the claimant failed the residency requirement check before Blake's specific circumstances could be evaluated. The claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements, which is a mandatory criterion for claiming Child Benefit. The claimant is living abroad for personal reasons and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not in a qualifying country. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child (Alex, age 6, and Blake, age 16) because the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. While Alex clearly meets the child-related criteria, and Blake's situation was not fully assessed, the residency requirement applies to the claimant regardless of the children's circumstances. The claimant is living abroad for personal reasons without meeting any of the special circumstances that would allow claiming Child Benefit from outside the UK (such as being a Crown servant, having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or living in certain qualifying countries).

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
